The '''repetitive''' aspect in Aelaki expresses re-doing a cessation -- stopping something again that had been resumed. It is one of the [[Stative aspects|stative verb aspects]], formed with the prefix '''li-''' combined with '''umlaut''' of the verb stem.

== Formation ==

The repetitive is formed by prefixing '''li-''' to the umlaut stem of the stative verb.

{| class="wikitable"
! Stative verb !! Repetitive !! Meaning
|-
| zoduk (know) || lizedik || re-forget (stop knowing again)
|}

== Semantic scope ==

The repetitive indicates that a [[Cessative|cessation]] is happening again. Where the [[Cessative|cessative]] ''nïzedik'' means "forget" (stop knowing), and the [[Resumptive|resumptive]] ''luzedik'' means "remember" (resume knowing), the repetitive ''lizedik'' means "re-forget" -- to stop knowing again after having remembered.

This creates a natural cycle of stative aspect operations:

# [[Inchoative]] ''nüzoduk'' -- learn (start knowing)
# [[Cessative]] ''nïzedik'' -- forget (stop knowing)
# [[Resumptive]] ''luzedik'' -- remember (resume knowing)
# '''Repetitive''' ''lizedik'' -- re-forget (stop knowing again)

== Relationship to other stative aspects ==

{| class="wikitable"
! Prefix !! Aspect !! Example !! Meaning
|-
| ho- || [[Probable]] || hozoduk || curious (about to know)
|-
| nü- || [[Inchoative]] || nüzoduk || learn (start to know)
|-
| nï- (+ umlaut) || [[Cessative]] || nïzedik || forget (stop knowing)
|-
| lu- || [[Resumptive]] || luzedik || remember (resume knowing)
|-
| '''li-''' (+ umlaut) || '''Repetitive''' || '''lizedik''' || '''re-forget (stop knowing again)'''
|-
| ko- || [[Almost]] || kozoduk || almost learn
|}

== See also ==

* [[Cessative]]
* [[Resumptive]]
* [[Inchoative]]

[[Category:grammatical aspects]]
[[Category:Stative aspects]]
